title: 5 Easy Cookie Dough Scoop Recipes for Busy Weeknights
keyword: easy cookie dough scoop recipes weeknight meals
meta_description: Discover 5 easy cookie dough scoop recipes perfect for busy weeknights. Learn how the Cookie Dough Scoop saves time and guarantees uniform, delicious results ev
html:
When you need a quick dinner that feels indulgent, a handful of cookie‑style bites can be the answer. Using a reliable Cookie Dough Scoop makes it simple to portion out perfectly uniform cookies, meatballs, or muffins in seconds. Below are five easy cookie dough scoop recipes that fit right into a weeknight routine without sacrificing flavor.

1. Classic Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough

This is the go‑to recipe for any cookie lover. The dough holds its shape when scooped, so the Cookie Dough Scoop delivers evenly sized cookies that bake uniformly.

  • 1 ½ cups all‑purpose flour
  • ½ teaspoon baking soda
  • ¼ teaspoon salt
  • ½ cup unsalted butter, softened
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ¼ cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Mix dry ingredients in a bowl. In a separate bowl, cream butter and sugars, then add egg and vanilla. Combine the two mixtures, fold in chocolate chips, and chill for 20 minutes. Using the Cookie Dough Scoop (size 1¼‑inch works well), drop dough onto a parchment‑lined sheet and bake at 350°F for 10‑12 minutes.

2. Peanut Butter Oatmeal Cookies

Protein‑packed and ready in under 30 minutes, these cookies are perfect for a quick snack after work. The scoop ensures each cookie gets the same amount of oats, which prevents some from turning soggy while others over‑bake.

Ingredients:

  1. 1 cup natural peanut butter
  2. ½ cup honey
  3. 1 egg
  4. 1 ½ cups rolled oats
  5. ½ cup raisins (optional)
  6. ¼ teaspoon baking powder

Blend peanut butter, honey, and egg until smooth. Stir in oats, baking powder, and raisins. Chill briefly, then scoop onto a baking sheet. Bake at 375°F for 8‑10 minutes. The cookies stay soft inside and develop a light crisp on the edges.

3. Savory Cheddar‑Bacon Mini Muffins

When dinner needs a side that feels like comfort food, these bite‑size muffins are a winner. Using the Cookie Dough Scoop (a ¾‑inch size is ideal) guarantees each muffin gets the same amount of batter, so they rise evenly.

Ingredients:

  • 1 cup all‑purpose flour
  • 1 teaspoon baking powder
  • ¼ teaspoon salt
  • ¼ cup milk
  • ¼ cup shredded sharp cheddar
  • 2 slices cooked bacon, crumbled
  • 1 egg, lightly beaten
  • 2 tablespoons melted butter

Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. In another bowl, combine milk, egg, and butter, then fold in cheese and bacon. Add dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Scoop batter into a greased mini‑muffin tin and bake at 375°F for 12‑14 minutes. Serve warm with a simple green salad.

4. One‑Pan Meatball‑And‑Veggie Dinner

Turn a classic meatball recipe into a weeknight dinner that needs only one pan. The Cookie Dough Scoop lets you portion meatballs quickly, giving each bite the same size for even cooking.

Ingredients for meatballs:

  1. 1 lb ground turkey or beef
  2. ½ cup breadcrumbs
  3. ¼ cup grated Parmesan
  4. 1 egg
  5. 2 cloves garlic, minced
  6. 1 teaspoon dried oregano
  7. Salt and pepper to taste

Mix all ingredients in a bowl. Using the Cookie Dough Scoop (size 1‑inch), drop meatballs onto a sheet pan. Surround with cut‑up veggies—such as broccoli florets, sliced carrots, and bell pepper strips. Drizzle everything with olive oil, season with salt, and roast at 400°F for 20‑25 minutes, turning the meatballs halfway through. The result is a balanced plate with protein and veggies ready in under 30 minutes.

5. Cinnamon Sugar Sweet Potato Bites

Sweet potatoes are a nutritious base for a quick, sweet bite. This recipe uses the Cookie Dough Scoop to shape the mashed sweet potatoes into uniform rounds that crisp up nicely.

Ingredients:

  • 2 large sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 2 tablespoons melted butter
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • Pinch of salt

Boil the sweet potatoes until fork‑tender, then mash with butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, and salt. Let the mash cool enough to handle, then scoop with a ½‑inch Cookie Dough Scoop onto a parchment‑lined sheet. Bake at 375°F for 12‑15 minutes, turning once, until the edges are golden. These bites make a satisfying dessert or a sweet side dish.

FAQ

Can I use the Cookie Dough Scoop for non‑baked items?

Absolutely. The scoop works great for portioning raw meatballs, cookie dough, or even pancake batter. Its sturdy design handles both sticky and firm mixtures.

What size scoop should I choose for a standard cookie?

A 1¼‑inch scoop produces cookies that are about 2‑inch in diameter after spreading, which is the classic size for most chocolate chip or oatmeal recipes.

Do I need to grease the scoop before using it?

No. The Cookie Dough Scoop’s smooth surface releases dough easily. If you’re working with an especially sticky dough, a quick spray of non‑stick cooking spray can help, but it’s rarely necessary.
